reasoning: The yfinance tool indicates a combined probability anchor of 0.56 based on seasonal and historical factors. The stock experienced a recent surge up to ~$125 on August 28-29, driven by stronger-than-expected Q3 2025 earnings and a raised full-year growth and revenue guidance. Federal Reserve rate cut signals also boost growth-oriented life science stocks like Agilent. Upward momentum and the prospect of a Post-Earnings Announcement Drift (PEAD) keep the short-term probability above 0.5. However, since stock returns are highly volatile over longer periods, uncertainty increases. Reversion to the mean and general unpredictability dictate that probabilities for more distant horizons should gradually decay toward 0.5.
